title: The founder chapter now rests on dated public releases
summary: February and August 2013 provided the first durable public record. These documents make Sean Xiang legible inside a real enterprise-infrastructure narrative before later validation stacks up.

comparativeBeats.label: Identity becomes public
comparativeBeats.title: OpenStack gives the chapter a named founder frame
comparativeBeats.text: The release states role, market problem, and company thesis in the same document.

comparativeBeats.label: Market texture appears
comparativeBeats.title: VMware shows the company targeting trust-heavy infrastructure
comparativeBeats.text: The buyer language was already about virtualization, cloud trust, and privacy risk.

comparativeBeats.label: Sequence
comparativeBeats.title: A founding-year sequence
comparativeBeats.text: A progression from role confirmation into ecosystem positioning.

title: The middle years read as a sequence of trust surfaces
summary: The chronicle shows how certification, alliance status, HSM trust, storage-networking interoperability, security validation, and marketplace distribution accumulate across several infrastructure waves.

comparativeBeats.label: 2014 · Entry
comparativeBeats.title: Dell certification opens the validation run with interoperability language
comparativeBeats.text: The company becomes legible through formal compatibility.

comparativeBeats.label: 2014 · Breadth
comparativeBeats.title: Hitachi widens the story from one platform into a broader storage ecosystem
comparativeBeats.text: The trust trail spreads across enterprise infrastructure categories.

comparativeBeats.label: 2016 · Security depth
comparativeBeats.title: Ultra Electronics AEP moves into key custody and HSM posture
comparativeBeats.text: The company speaks the language of hardened key storage and enterprise cryptographic architecture.

comparativeBeats.label: 2017 · Maturity
comparativeBeats.title: IBM marks sustained enterprise validation
comparativeBeats.text: Recognition continues after the first wave of ecosystem entry.

comparativeBeats.label: 2017 · Continuity
comparativeBeats.title: ATTO shows the trust trail touching storage-networking infrastructure
comparativeBeats.text: The story reaches the physical data path and performance-sensitive infrastructure surfaces.

comparativeBeats.label: 2019 · Translation
comparativeBeats.title: Azure Marketplace carries the validation story into public-cloud distribution
comparativeBeats.text: The same trust logic moves from storage and HSM ecosystems into cloud-era deployment surfaces.

title: Expansion logic
summary: Vancouver matters because the 2018 Launch Academy profile explains why Canada enters the story, while the Bloombase offices page proves the expansion sits inside a wider operating footprint.

comparativeBeats.label: Why
comparativeBeats.title: Canada enters the story
comparativeBeats.text: Talent, cryptography, and ecosystem positioning gave Vancouver strategic meaning.

comparativeBeats.label: Where
comparativeBeats.title: Part of a real operating network
comparativeBeats.text: The footprint is materially grounded and expands the global ecosystem.

title: A credible present-tense sequence: reframing, visibility, then alliance execution
summary: A 2023 technical reframing around NVIDIA DPUs and GPUs, ongoing public news visibility, and a 2025 alliance that explicitly names the AI era.

comparativeBeats.label: 2023 · Reframing
comparativeBeats.title: NVIDIA brings accelerated-computing language
comparativeBeats.text: The company explained how security primitives should be rebuilt for a new stack.

comparativeBeats.label: 2024 · Continuity
comparativeBeats.title: Ongoing visibility
comparativeBeats.text: Conference and news-surface visibility confirm persistent activity.

comparativeBeats.label: 2025 · Execution
comparativeBeats.title: Utimaco alliance
comparativeBeats.text: The alliance language itself names AI-era data security.
